title: Count-encoding refinement for confirmed legacy block
summary: Refine the confirmed useful count-encoding block with a compact transformed count feature.
rationale: Removing count encoding hurt performance, so frequency information matters. A small refinement can test whether log-counts or normalized frequencies are better behaved than raw counts.
implementation_hint: Start from the parent two-seed RealMLP average code. Keep the existing count-encoding block and add or replace exactly one count variant such as log1p(count), count / n_rows, or both raw count and log1p(count) for the same categorical columns. Do not add target encoding, new combo features, model-family changes, stacking, or calibration.
expected_effect: Improve OOF AUC if transformed frequency features provide a smoother signal than raw counts alone.
risk: Extra count variants may duplicate existing information and add noise.
